input_candidates.truth_value: Described in Equations (1)–(4). Precipitate strengthening dominant mechanism determined by comparing σ_order and σ_coh+σ_mod. Good agreement between predicted and experimental yield strengths.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fracture surfaces of all four steels exhibit numerous dimples, indicating ductile fracture mode (Fig. 5).
input_candidates.prediction_value: The total elongation of 2Ni5Cr steel is ~16% higher than that of the no-Cr 2Ni steel, while its yield strength decreases from 1202 MPa (2Ni) to 1071 MPa.

input_candidates.truth_value: Engineering stress-strain curves show that total elongation increases with Cr content, while yield strength increases with Ni content; the 2Ni3Cr steel achieves YS>1100 MPa, UTS>1200 MPa, TEL>45%, and a TS×TEL product of 55.36 GPa·%.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Compared to 3Cr steel, 2Ni3Cr steel shows a yield strength increase of ~141 MPa due to Ni addition.

input_candidates.truth_value: Strain hardening rate curves exhibit three stages: Stage I (sharp decrease), Stage II (increase, prolonged with Cr), Stage III (decrease); 2Ni5Cr shows higher hardening rate than 2Ni and 2Ni3Cr in Stage III.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Strain hardening curves (Fig. 4c) exhibit three stages: Stage I – sharp decrease in strain hardening rate; Stage II – increasing rate, prolonged with increasing Cr content among 2Ni/2Ni3Cr/2Ni5Cr; Stage III – decreasing rate, with 2Ni5Cr > 2Ni3Cr > 2Ni in work hardening rate.

input_candidates.truth_value: Deformation mechanisms differ: 3Cr steel shows HDDA and dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR) with few deformation twins near fracture; 2Ni steel is dominated by planar slip, no deformation twins, only stacking faults at very high strain.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Deformation substructure of 3Cr steel near final failure (54% strain) shows high-density dislocation arrays (HDDA), very fine slip bands, deformation twins, and stacking faults; twins appear only near fracture surfaces with limited contribution to overall ductility.

input_candidates.truth_value: First-principles calculations indicate that Cr addition increases formation energy of κ-carbides (e.g., Cr21(FeC3)2 formation energy -0.049 eV/atom vs. -0.160 eV/atom for ideal (Fe,Mn)3AlC), thereby suppressing nucleation and growth, while Ni addition reduces formation energy (AlNi3C formation energy -0.181 eV/atom), promoting κ-carbide formation.
input_candidates.prediction_value: 2Ni steel deformation is dominated by planar slip, slip band multiplication, and dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR); no deformation twins detected throughout plastic deformation; stacking faults appear only at very high strain near final failure.

input_candidates.truth_value: Comparing with other steels (Table 2, Fig. 4d), the present Ni&Cr-alloyed Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.5C steels achieve a superior combination of yield strength and total elongation, notably higher yield strength at similar GPa·% levels.
input_candidates.prediction_value: First-principles formation energies: (Fe,Mn)3AlC κ-carbide = -0.160 eV/atom; Cr21(FeC3)2 = -0.049 eV/atom (0.111 eV/atom higher than (Fe,Mn)3AlC); AlNi3C = -0.181 eV/atom; Fe2NiC = +0.082 eV/atom. Cr increases nucleation energy barrier, suppressing κ-carbide growth and favoring LRO domains; Ni reduces barrier, promoting κ-carbide formation via AlNi3C.

input_candidates.truth_value: The shear modulus difference between austenite matrix and κ-carbides is estimated as ΔG ≈ 23.3 GPa; the transition radius where dominant precipitation strengthening shifts from ordering to coherency+modulus mismatch is indicated in Fig. 10.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Calculated strengthening contributions: σ0 = 42 MPa; σgb = 238.5 MPa (3Cr), 231.3 MPa (2Ni), 231.7 MPa (2Ni3Cr), 301.2 MPa (2Ni5Cr); σss = 515.4 MPa (3Cr), 509.5 MPa (2Ni), 557.2 MPa (2Ni3Cr), 554.1 MPa (2Ni5Cr); σprep varies with precipitate size distribution.

input_candidates.truth_value: Hall-Petch coefficient for these steels is taken as KHP = 446.8 MPa·μm^0.5; effective grain sizes (including twins) used for calculation are 3.51, 3.73, 3.72, and 2.2 μm respectively.

candidates.truth_value: Deformation mechanisms differ: 3Cr steel shows HDDA and dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR) with few deformation twins near fracture; 2Ni steel is dominated by planar slip, no deformation twins, only stacking faults at very high strain.

candidates.truth_value: First-principles calculations indicate that Cr addition increases formation energy of κ-carbides (e.g., Cr21(FeC3)2 formation energy -0.049 eV/atom vs. -0.160 eV/atom for ideal (Fe,Mn)3AlC), thereby suppressing nucleation and growth, while Ni addition reduces formation energy (AlNi3C formation energy -0.181 eV/atom), promoting κ-carbide formation.

candidates.prediction_normalized: 2ni steel deformation is dominated by planar slip, slip band multiplication, and dynamic slip band refinement (dsbr); no deformation twins detected throughout plastic deformation; stacking faults appear only at very high strain near final failure.
